summary: First-finish smoke test of Daily Planner. Backend auth + planner CRUD endpoints all functional (login, register, me, planner GET/PUT, dates). Frontend loads, protected redirect works, dashboard renders all 13 card headers, date-picker/save/logout testids present. However a critical UI regression prevents users from entering data into most cards: for a fresh date the backend returns empty arrays which overwrite the frontend defaults in DashboardPage loadDay, so priority/todo/appointment/personal_todo/contact/expense INPUT ROWS never render (only headers show). Water tracker, notes, tomorrow notes and rating still work.
passed: POST /api/auth/register new user -> 200 (sets cookies, logs in)
Data isolation: new user's GET /api/planner/2025-11-15 returns empty (not admin's saved data)
Frontend: admin login redirects to / (dashboard) and shows 'Hi, Admin'
Frontend: date-picker-trigger, save-planner-button, logout-button, notes-textarea, water-drop-0, expense-total testids present
Frontend: logout button clears session and returns to /login
Frontend: registration form on /register works end-to-end and lands on dashboard

failed.issue: For any date where the backend returns empty arrays (top_priorities:[], todo_list:[], appointments:[], personal_todo:[], contacts:[], expenses:[]) the merge `setData({ ...emptyPlannerData(), ...res.data })` OVERWRITES the seeded default rows with the empty arrays. As a result the Top Priorities, To Do List, Appointments, Personal To-Do, Calls/Email/Text and Expense Tracker cards render only their header/tape — no input rows exist for the user to type into. Users have no way to enter priorities, todos, appointments, or expenses (except via the single add-expense-button).
failed.evidence: Screenshot after admin login shows Top Priorities / To Do List / Personal To-Do cards visually empty with no <input>. Playwright: locator('[data-testid="priority-input-0"]').count() == 0, locator('[data-testid="todo-input-0"]').count() == 0, locator('[data-testid="expense-item-0"]').count() == 0 while water-drop-0, notes-textarea, expense-total ARE present. Code: /app/frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.jsx line 31 `setData({ ...emptyPlannerData(), ...res.data })` combined with backend PlannerData default_factory=list at /app/backend/server.py.

failed.issue: POST /api/auth/logout returns {ok:true} but the Set-Cookie deletion headers do not appear to unset the access_token cookie in curl's jar (cookie value remains and JWT is still usable until natural expiry). In browsers the logout works (verified via Playwright — user is bounced to /login and cannot access dashboard).
failed.evidence: curl -c jar; POST /api/auth/logout; jar still contains access_token JWT that continues to authenticate GET /api/auth/me.

action_items: Fix DashboardPage loadDay merge so empty arrays from backend do NOT wipe default input rows. Suggested: deep-merge or replace empty arrays with defaults, e.g. `const d = res.data; const def = emptyPlannerData(); setData({ ...def, ...d, top_priorities: d.top_priorities?.length ? d.top_priorities : def.top_priorities, todo_list: d.todo_list?.length ? d.todo_list : def.todo_list, appointments: ..., personal_todo: ..., contacts: ..., expenses: ... });` Alternative: have backend GET /api/planner/{date} return the same default rows (3 priorities, 8 todos, etc.) when a record does not yet exist.
Optionally add explicit 'Add row' buttons for lists so users can extend beyond defaults.
Verify backend logout uses response.delete_cookie(...) with the same path/domain/secure/samesite=None used at set time so browsers/curl consistently drop the cookie.
